title: desmodium podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum
intro: Desmodium podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum, also known as Ox-tongue tick trefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Desmodium podocarpum subsp. oxyphyllum can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
